heaviest_rainfall.status: The largest amount of rain ever recorded is on 09 November 2025 when 47.89mm fell at a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour over the 144.8 hour event.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The rain rate reached 34.3mm per hour on 02 September 2025, and resulted in 21.19mm of rain over 15.0 hours.  This is the highest rate recorded by our weather station.

longest_rain.status: The lengthiest precipitation event recorded spanned 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025, it was characterized by a peak rain intensity of 24.6mm per hour and a total rainfall of 47.89mm.

most_lightning.status: The most lightning activity recorded in a single day is 23, which occurred on 07 September 2025 (since our records began on 11 April 2025).

storm.status: No stormy weather is anticipated.  The last big storm was recorded on 21 December 2025, lasting 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 26 November 2025 and lasted for 14 days.

cold_snaps.status: Currently, the weather is stable with no cold snap in progress.  So far, 6 cold snaps have been recorded this year.  The average yearly count is 3.7.

extreme.status: The highest recorded temperature difference is 26.7°C from 29 April 2025 (when the temperature reached 27.1°C but fell to 0.4°C).

heatwaves.status: There is not an active heatwaves.  There has been 6 so far this year, while the average is 1.9.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was recorded on 18 July 2025.  The total number of occurrences since 1973 number is only 62.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (195°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: Currently, this is the 2nd highest wind gust on record with 91.4 mph.  The top wind gust was 93.7 on 13 October 2012.

run.status: The largest wind run ever was on 21 October 2012 with a value of 1737 miles.

speed.status: The top recorded wind speed is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The ongoing windy stretch, 3 days and counting started on 23 December 2025 and ranks 7th in length since records started in 23 September 2024.  The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the feels like temperature (down 2.4°C), outside temperature (down 1.5°C), and inside temperature (down 1.5°C) and drier conditions in outside humidity (down 5%).

The past week and month had observable variation in outside temperature (up 4.5°C), inside temperature (up 2.8°C), and outside humidity (up 15%) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 361.1m higher than yesterday, 825.5m higher than the past week, 990.4m higher than the past month.

The cloud levels yesterday fell between 1081m minimum and 2020m maximum, with an average of 1878.5m; weekly and monthly averages were marginally higher at 1053.1m and 888.1m.
cloud levels.summary: Clouds will be high, allowing good sunlight.

summary.summary: Inside; on the cooler side. Outside; icy, freezing temperatures. Also, the air will feel dry and mostly calm with light air movement. Possible light showers, clouds will be high, allowing good sunlight, and dim or diffuse light conditions. Stable conditions are expected under a high pressure system.

washing.phrase: The current temperature is too low (2.8°C) for optimal drying conditions.  Any drying will take longer.
